task: Doc Truth Analysis — Phase 2 atom A: reconcile the ROOT README.md and CONTRIBUTING file against current code. Goal: every load-bearing claim in these two root docs matches the actual repo (commands, scripts, CI gates, paths, workflow, package layout). SCOPE — files you may edit: README.md and CONTRIBUTING* at the repo root ONLY (plus any root PR/issue templates these two reference, e.g. .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE*). Do NOT edit cocoder/** (governance — Oscar-lane), do NOT edit product code, do NOT touch docs/ design briefs in this atom. METHOD: (1) Read README.md and CONTRIBUTING end to end. (2) For each concrete claim — build/test/lint commands, script names, CI checks, required tooling, directory layout, contribution workflow — verify it against the live tree (package.json scripts, .github/workflows, actual paths). (3) Where the DOC is wrong, fix the doc to match code. (4) Known issue to resolve: open ticket 0037 flags a stale CONTRIBUTING/PR-template `rg` CI-gate claim — confirm against .github/workflows and the PR template, and correct the doc text to current reality. (5) Where the CODE appears wrong (doc describes intended behavior that code does not implement), do NOT change code and do NOT silently edit the doc — instead collect it. DELIVERABLE: (a) the corrected README/CONTRIBUTING diff; (b) a discrepancy inventory as a markdown list in your final report, each row: claim → location (file:line) → verdict (DOC-WRONG-fixed / CODE-WRONG-surfaced / OK) → evidence (file/path/command). Report any CODE-WRONG findings explicitly so Oscar can file a ticket — you cannot write cocoder/tickets/**. MUST NOT BREAK: do not delete content that is still accurate; do not reformat unrelated sections; preserve heading structure. Acceptance: README and CONTRIBUTING contain no claim contradicting current code, ticket-0037's stale rg-gate text is corrected with evidence, and a complete discrepancy inventory is returned.

Doc Truth Analysis — Phase 2 atom B: audit the DESIGN-INTENT briefs under docs/ for STALE PATH/IDENTIFIER references ONLY. SCOPE — files you may edit (docs/** only): docs/oz-design-brief.md, docs/oz-streaming-design.md, docs/ui-dev-notes.md, docs/oscar-deb-repair-dialogue-design.md, docs/founder-brief-format-durability.md, docs/research/oz-flat-file-access.md. Do NOT edit any other file; do NOT touch product code, cocoder/**, README/CONTRIBUTING, or the other docs/ files. CRITICAL FRAMING: these are DESIGN-INTENT / aspirational documents. You are NOT reconciling their design against current behavior and you must NOT flag a not-yet-built or differently-built design as 'wrong'. The ONLY discrepancy class in scope is a STALE concrete reference that no longer resolves: a file path, directory, module, script, CLI command/subcommand, package name, ADR number, or config-file name that the doc cites as if it exists today but which has moved or been renamed/deleted. METHOD: for each doc, extract every concrete path/identifier reference and check it against the live tree (ls/grep the real path, package.json scripts, .github/workflows, cocoder/decisions for ADR numbers, cocoder/personas/assignments.json, etc.). When a referenced path is stale AND an unambiguous current equivalent exists, update the reference to the current path. If a path is stale but you cannot confidently map it to a current equivalent, OR you are unsure whether something is a stale path vs an intended-future artifact, DO NOT edit it — list it as a question for Oscar instead. Leave all prose describing intended/aspirational behavior untouched. DELIVERABLE: (a) the corrected diff (path-ref fixes only); (b) a reference inventory in your final report — each row: doc:line → referenced path/id → verdict (STALE-fixed / OK / UNSURE-surfaced) → evidence (the live path you checked). MUST NOT BREAK: do not rewrite design narrative, do not reformat sections, do not delete aspirational content. Acceptance: every concrete path/identifier in these six docs either resolves against the live tree or is explicitly surfaced as UNSURE for Oscar; no aspirational design flagged as wrong; complete reference inventory returned.

Doc Truth Analysis — Phase 2 atom C: reconcile the REMAINING un-audited docs/ files against live code. SCOPE — files you may edit (docs/** only): docs/faq.md, docs/oz.md, docs/oz-launch.md, docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md, docs/dogfood-evidence.md, docs/fault-injection-live-proofs.md, docs/loop-packets-dispatch-inventory.md, docs/loop-packets-retrofit-audit.md — PLUS docs/docs-files-truth-audit.md (the worklist, to append your findings). Do NOT edit any other docs/ file (the other ~19 were already audited in run_267 / earlier atoms), do NOT edit product code, cocoder/**, README/CONTRIBUTING, or ARCHITECTURE.md. METHOD per file: (1) read it; (2) for every CONCRETE current-truth claim and every path/module/CLI/script/ADR/test reference, verify against the live tree (ls/grep real paths, package.json scripts, .github/workflows, cocoder/decisions for ADR numbers, packages/** for modules/tests). (3) Where the DOC is wrong about current reality (stale path, renamed module, removed test, changed command, wrong owner), fix the doc to match code. (4) Where the CODE appears wrong (doc describes intended behavior code does not implement), do NOT change code and do NOT silently edit the doc — collect it as a CODE-WRONG finding for Oscar to ticket (you cannot write cocoder/tickets/**). DISCIPLINE — these eight include several EVIDENCE / OWNER-MAP / AUDIT artifacts (oz-hardening-owner-map, dogfood-evidence, fault-injection-live-proofs, loop-packets-*): treat their line-anchored historical evidence the way run_267 did — do NOT flag genuinely historical/superseded evidence or aspirational design as 'wrong'; only reconcile references the doc presents as CURRENT owner maps / active paths. If a doc is wholly historical, add/confirm a historical banner rather than rewriting its evidence. If unsure whether something is stale-current vs intended-historical, surface it to Oscar rather than editing. (5) WORKLIST: append a Coverage line per file and a Discrepancies-table row per finding to docs/docs-files-truth-audit.md, matching that file's existing column format (# | file+location | claim/reference | evidence checked path:line or `not found` | verdict ACCURATE/STALE/WRONG/UNVERIFIABLE | proposed fix direction / resolution). DELIVERABLE: the corrected diff plus a summary list of each finding (file:line → verdict → resolution) and any CODE-WRONG items surfaced for Oscar. MUST NOT BREAK: do not delete accurate content, do not reformat unrelated sections, preserve heading structure and the worklist's existing table shape. Acceptance: each of the eight docs has every concrete current-truth claim/reference either confirmed against live code or fixed (doc-wrong) or surfaced (code-wrong/unsure); historical evidence not mis-flagged; docs-files-truth-audit.md updated with Coverage + Discrepancy rows carrying verdict + resolution for every item.
